checks.a_analysis: The output accurately reflects the paragraphs and sentences found in the source document. Every 'WHEREAS' clause and the 'NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED' section are present exactly as written in the source. There is no evidence of hallucinated or invented text.
The source document does not contain any contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs, or addresses). The output also contains no such information, thus no fabrication has occurred.

The source document is a single-page resolution. All components, including the headers, title, five 'WHEREAS' clauses, the resolution statement, the date, and the names/titles of the signers, are successfully captured in the output.
There are no tables in the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section of the resolution is fully transcribed. No key names, dates, or legislative references (such as the 9-1-1 SAVES Act or the SOC catalog) have been omitted. The signers' names and titles are also fully present.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output maintains high fidelity to the source text. Punctuation, spelling, and proper nouns are all correct. It correctly differentiates between '9-1-1' and '911' exactly as used in the source. The formatting of the date with superscript 'th' is an acceptable minor reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the source document with the provided JSON output. All text blocks, including the header, title, preamble clauses, resolution, and signature blocks, appear exactly once in the output, mirroring the source. There is no evidence of duplicated content.

The document title 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF ROCKINGHAM COUNTY 911 EMERGENCY TELECOMMUNICATORS' is correctly identified as a heading (level 2). While titles are often level 1, the acceptable changes allow for level normalization for screen reader navigation. No heading levels are skipped.
The source document does not contain any tables. The JSON output correctly reflects this with a table count of 0.

The JSON output follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right reading order of the source document: starting with the location header, followed by the resolution title, the series of 'WHEREAS' clauses, the 'NOW, THEREFORE' resolution statement, the date, and finally the signature blocks and official seal. This preserves the document's flow and meaning.

The source document does not contain explicit bulleted or numbered lists. The parallel 'WHEREAS' clauses are correctly rendered as individual paragraphs, which is faithful to the source's visual structure.
The source document contains no hyperlinks or URLs. No links were added to the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and layout of the source document. The centered headers, sequential paragraphs, signature blocks, and placement of the seal in the second rendering block align with the original PDF. While there is a duplication of content, the intended visual structure and flow of the resolution are faithfully represented.
The source document does not contain any tables, so this check is not applicable.
The meaningful visual elements from the source, specifically the Rockingham County seal and the handwritten signatures, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

The entire body content of the resolution is duplicated in the HTML output. After the initial text-only version of the resolution (starting from 'NORTH CAROLINA ROCKINGHAM COUNTY' and ending with 'CLERK TO THE BOARD'), the document repeats in its entirety with full styling and images. This results in every 'WHEREAS' clause and the 'NOW, THEREFORE' clause appearing twice on a single page.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text of the resolution, including paragraphs beginning with 'WHEREAS' and 'NOW, THEREFORE', appears only once.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text of the resolution is repeated. For example, the paragraph starting with 'WHEREAS, in September of 2021...' appears at the top of the content area and again further down after the first signature block.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the body of the document.
